name: Shanghai Cooperation Organisation
climate: Ranges from tropical monsoon (South Asia) to subarctic (Siberia); arid continental in Central Asia; temperate in eastern China

neighbors.name: Belt and Road Initiative
neighbors.relevance: BRI's overland corridors traverse SCO member states; SCO provides the security framework for BRI logistics

neighbors.name: Commonwealth of Independent States
neighbors.relevance: Most Central Asian SCO members are also CIS members; overlapping post-Soviet integration

neighbors.name: BRICS
neighbors.relevance: China, Russia, and India are in both blocs; SCO and BRICS together form the core of multipolar institution-building

neighbors.name: OPEC+
neighbors.relevance: Russia, Kazakhstan, and Iran are OPEC+ producers; energy policy coordination overlaps with SCO agenda

neighbors.name: Strait of Hormuz
neighbors.relevance: Iran's SCO membership makes Hormuz energy transit a direct concern for the organization

neighbors.name: Persian Gulf
neighbors.relevance: Iran's inclusion ties SCO to Gulf energy dynamics and regional security

links.description: SCO provides security and diplomatic framework for BRI's Central Asian overland corridors
China, Russia, India share membership; joint push for multipolar alternatives to Western institutions
Russia, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Uzbekistan are members of both SCO and CIS
SCO energy producers (Russia, Kazakhstan, Iran) coordinate output via OPEC+
SCO is often viewed as a counterweight to NATO expansion in Eurasia
Iran's membership links SCO energy security to Hormuz transit stability
China bridges SCO and RCEP, connecting Central Asian resources to Asia-Pacific markets
